senses.definition: A brass musical instrument that combines features of the saxhorn family and cornets; often used in brass ensembles.
examples: The band included a rare saxcornet that blended the tones of a saxhorn and a cornet.
They showcased the saxcornet in the brass section for a smoother fusion sound.

senses.definition: A proper noun referring to a historical title (comte de Saxe) or region associated with Saxony; also used as a surname.
examples: Hermann Maurice Saxe was a famous French marshal.
Saxe is the region around the Elbe in Germany.

senses.definition: A surname or place name historically associated with Saxony or related titles.
examples: He traced his lineage to the Saxe family.
The Saxe family played a role in European military history.

senses.definition: Plural of sax, referring to a family of brass instruments or to a person named Saxe; can also be a plural proper noun in historical discussions.
examples: The saxes were used in the band during the parade.
Historians discuss the Saxes in early Germanic regions.

senses.definition: A family of valved brass instruments invented by Adolphe Sax, varying from high-pitched to low-pitched instruments used in military bands and orchestras.
examples: The saxhorn family includes several sizes perfect for brass ensembles.
A rare saxhorn was played in the antique brass band.

senses.definition: Plural form of saxhorn; refiere to multiple instruments from the saxhorn family.
examples: The band acquired several new saxhorns for the outdoor concert.
Collectors seek vintage saxhorns from the 19th century.

senses.definition: A genus of marine bivalve molluscs that boring into hard substrates.
examples: Saxicava species are known for boring into rocky shores.
Scientists study Saxicava to understand boring molluscs.

senses.definition: Relating to Saxicava or characterized by boring into substrates (used in biology).
examples: The saxicavous behavior was observed in the soft rock samples.
Saxicavous organisms create permanent bore holes.

senses.definition: A genus of birds in the European robin family; commonly known as stonechats.
examples: Saxicola torquatus is a well-known species in Europe.
Birdwatchers often spot saxicolas near heathlands.

senses.definition: Growing or living in rocks or stone; rock-dwelling.
examples: Saxicole plants cling to the cliff faces.
Saxicole habitats are challenging for researchers.

senses.definition: The family Saxicolidae, a biological taxonomic group.
examples: Saxicolidae includes several rock-dwelling species.
Taxonomists classified the specimen within Saxicolidae.

senses.definition: A subfamily within Saxicolidae or related rock-dwelling taxa.
examples: Saxicolinae is studied for its unique adaptations to rocky habitats.
The subfamily Saxicolinae includes several genera.

senses.definition: Of or relating to rocks or rock-dwelling; rocky.
examples: The saxicoline habitat is full of crevices and ledges.
Saxicoline species often thrive in cliff environments.

senses.definition: Dwelling on rocks or rock surfaces; rock-dwelling.
examples: Saxicolous plants grow on rocky slopes.
The saxicolous lichen covers the cliff face.

senses.definition: A genus of flowering plants commonly known as Saxifrages, found in rocky habitats.
examples: Saxifraga species adorn alpine rock gardens.
The saxifraga thrives in cool, shaded crevices.

senses.definition: The plant family Saxifragaceae, which includes the genus Saxifraga.
examples: Saxifragaceae include several alpine ornamentals.
Horticulturists study the Saxifragaceae family for rock gardens.

senses.definition: Of or belonging to the Saxifragaceae family or the genus Saxifraga.
examples: The saxifragaceous plants decorate the alpine garden.
Researchers studied the saxifragaceous traits.

senses.definition: A plant that bears saxifrages; sometimes used to describe a plant with saxifragoid characteristics.
examples: The saxifragant flourished in the rocky niche.
Gardeners labeled the plant as a saxifragant.

senses.definition: A flowering plant in the Saxifraga genus; common saxifrage blanket the rocky areas.
examples: Saxifrages blanket the alpine slopes in spring.
Saxifrage is a popular rock garden plant.

senses.definition: Plural of saxifrage; multiple saxifrages or species within Saxifraga.
examples: The rock garden features several saxifrages.
Saxifrages thrive in thin, rocky soil.

senses.definition: Resembling or characteristic of saxifrages; saxifrage-like.
examples: The saxifragous rock garden had many small, hardy plants.
A saxifragous growth pattern was observed on the cliff.

senses.definition: A historical or taxonomic term related to saxifrages; used in some botanical classifications.
examples: Researchers noted saxifrax in the old taxonomy papers.
The term saxifrax appeared in early botanical texts.

senses.definition: Originating from or pertaining to Saxon people or Saxony; sometimes used in geology to describe rock that has Saxon origins.
examples: The saxigenous rocks show ancient formations.
Saxigenous influences are noted in the region's geology.

senses.definition: Relating to or resembling a sax (a type of saxophone) or its style.
examples: The saxish timbre reminded listeners of classic jazz.
A saxish tone filled the nightclub.

senses.definition: A person who plays the saxophone; a saxophonist.
examples: He is a talented saxist in the jazz quartet.
The saxist played a soulful solo.

senses.definition: Plural of saxist; multiple saxophonists.
examples: The festival featured several renowned saxists.
Two saxists shared the stage for a duet.
